### 17. **docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-19-drone-relief-design.md**

**Master design spec for the full project:**

**1. Problem & Goal:**

Mobile app simulating drone-based delivery of relief supplies to civilians impacted by war. Two role-switched portals inside single Flutter codebase:
- **User portal** â request supplies, watch assigned drone fly in real time, confirm receipt
- **Admin portal** â manage requests, dispatch drones, monitor fleet, control simulated weather + inventory

Drone fleet **fully simulated** â no real flight hardware. Each drone: 15 km radius reachable in ~1 hour under clear weather; bad weather slows it; battery + mechanical faults can abort flights.

**Goals:**
- Realistic end-to-end delivery flow under multi-user, real-time conditions
- Demonstrable failure modes (weather/battery/mechanical) drive admin reassignment
- Server-of-truth state in Firestore; all clients see same drone position + queue
- Auditable Claude Code usage (per-dev session logs in repo) per professor's requirement

**Non-goals (explicit cut for 2 weeks):**
- Real weather API integration
- Real SMS/OTP auth
- Real payment / fee / scoring
- Admin analytics dashboard, audit log
- iOS push notifications (Android FCM only)
- KYC / national ID government verification

**2. Tech Stack Rationale:**

| Layer | Choice | Why |
|---|---|---|
| Mobile | Flutter | One codebase, Android primary, iOS stretch |
| State management | Riverpod | Modern, testable, supports async + caching |
| Routing | go_router | Declarative, deeply integrated with Flutter ecosystem |
| Map | flutter_map + OpenStreetMap | No API key, open data, performant for 8-12 drones |
| Auth | Firebase Auth + synthetic email | No OTP complexity; uses email/password with `<id>@drone-aid.local` |
| Database | Firestore | Realtime, document-oriented, works with emulator |
| Backend logic | Cloud Functions (TypeScript) | Callable RPC + scheduled cron + Firestore triggers natively |
| Push | FCM | Google-owned, integrated with Firebase |
| Scheduled jobs | Cloud Functions `onSchedule` | Native, no separate scheduler |
| Local dev | Firebase Emulator Suite | All-in-one, offline, no cloud credentials needed |
| CI/CD | GitHub Actions | Free, integrated, easy |

Why not Hono/Elysia on Cloud Run: Functions covers callable + cron + triggers natively; Firestore covers realtime; pulling in self-hosted server adds Docker + WebSocket plumbing that 2-week scope can't absorb.

**3. Architecture:**
```
Flutter app (single APK/IPA)
  USER screens | ADMIN screens | SHARED (auth, profile, settings)
       âââââââââââââââŹâââââââââââââââ
                Repository layer
                     â
              Riverpod providers
                     â
       Firebase SDK (Auth / Firestore / FCM / Functions)
                     â
                     âŒ
âââââââââââââââââ  FIREBASE  âââââââââââââââââ
 Auth        Firestore        Cloud Functions      FCM
                                  callable: 9 functions
                                  scheduled: tickFlights
                                  triggers: onFlightWritten
```

**Key decisions:**
- **One Flutter codebase, role-switched** on `users/{uid}.role`
- **Repository layer** wraps Firestore (allows mocks in widget tests)
- **Drone position computed client-side** from `flightPlan` (origin, destination, takeoffAt, speedKmh, weatherModifier) â eliminates per-second writes
- **State-changing admin actions through callable Cloud Functions** â keeps rules simple, centralizes business logic + atomic transactions

**4. Data Model (Firestore):**

Collections:
- **users/{uid}:** uid, nationalId (unique), name, phone, role, deliveryAddress, locked, fcmTokens[], createdAt
  - Sub: `users/{uid}/notifications/{nid}`: type, title, body, requestId?, flightId?, readAt, createdAt
- **catalog/{itemId}:** name, weightKg, stock, icon, active
- **requests/{reqId}:** userId, items[], totalWeightKg, deliveryAddress, priority, status, notes, decidedBy, decidedAt, rejectReason, currentFlightId, createdAt
- **drones/{droneId}:** name, status, batteryPct, baseLocation, maxPayloadKg, currentFlightId, lastSeenAt
- **flights/{flightId}:** droneId, requestId, userId, status, origin, destination, takeoffAt, etaAt, speedKmh, weatherModifierAtTakeoff, batteryAtTakeoff, failureType, tickHistory[]
- **weather/current:** state (clear|wind|storm), updatedBy, updatedAt

**Modeling notes:**
- Flight separate from request (request = "what user wants", flight = "delivery attempt")
- Snapshot address on request so original target retained if user moves pin later
- Stock lives on catalog, decremented atomically inside `approveRequest`
- Position derived, not stored (saves ~3,600 writes/hour per active drone)

**5. Auth, Roles, Identity:**

Registration form: national ID (13 digits + checksum validated), password, name, phone, optional delivery pin.

Firebase Auth uses synthetic email `<id>@drone-aid.local` â email/password provider works without real email.

On first sign-in, Cloud Function trigger (`onUserCreated`) provisions `users/{uid}` with `role: "user"`.

Admin accounts seeded via `seedAdmins.ts` script (one-time, idempotent).

ID checksum logic in `app/lib/utils/thai_id_validator.dart` AND `functions/src/lib/id.ts`; both tested.

**6. Pages:**

**User portal (8 screens):**
1. Login / Register â ID + password
2. Home / Request â catalog browse, qty picker, address pin, submit
3. Queue â own requests, live status
4. Tracking â live map of your drone, position interpolated client-side
5. Confirm â appears when status == `delivered`
6. History â completed / failed / cancelled requests
7. Notifications â in-app inbox
8. Profile + Settings â edit name, phone, pin, language, logout

**Admin portal (7 screens):**
1. Control (live map) â all currently flying drones
2. Drone list â all drones incl. offline + maintenance
3. Drone detail â status, battery, current flight, future queue
4. Requests â list of incoming, filters (status, priority)
5. Request manage â open request, see user profile, approve/reject/assign drone
6. Weather panel â set global weather (clear / wind / storm)
7. Supply inventory â list items, restock, toggle active

**7. End-to-end flow:**

USER submits â `submitRequest` validates + creates request (pending) â FCM ping admins â ADMIN approves + picks drone â `assignDrone` creates flight (enroute), drone flying â FCM ping user â USER watches Tracking page (client interpolation) â every 60s `tickFlights` ticks: no failure + progress >= 1.0 â flight.status = delivering â completed â FCM ping user "confirm please" â USER taps Confirm â `confirmDelivery` sets request.status = confirmed + flight.status = returning â tickFlights brings drone home â drone.status = idle.

Failure branch: `tickFlights` rolls failureType â flight.status = aborted â request.status = failed â drone.status = idle (if battery > 0) | maintenance â FCM ping user + admin â ADMIN reassigns new drone.

**8. Drone simulator engine:**

**Scheduled tick (every 60s):**
```
tickFlights():
  weather = read weather/current
  flights = query flights where status in [enroute, delivering, returning]

  for f in flights:
    elapsed_hr = (now - f.takeoffAt) / 3600
    distance_km = haversine(f.origin, f.destination)
    effective_speed = f.speedKmh Ă weatherMod(weather, f.status)
    progress = clamp(elapsed_hr Ă effective_speed / distance_km, 0, 1)
    battery = f.batteryAtTakeoff â elapsed_hr Ă DRAIN_PER_HR(weather)

    if f.status == enroute:
      if weather == storm AND rand() < 0.20 â abort("weather")
      if battery < 15 â abort("battery")
      if rand() < 0.01 â abort("mechanical")

    if aborted:
      f.failureType, f.status = aborted | failed
      request.status = failed
      drone.status = idle | maintenance
      notify(user, admin)

    elif progress >= 1.0 AND f.status == enroute:
      f.status = delivering (60s soft hold for "landing")
      notify(user, "drone arriving")

    elif progress >= 1.0 AND f.status == delivering:
      f.status = completed
      request.status = delivered
      notify(user, "please confirm")

    elif progress >= 1.0 AND f.status == returning:
      drone.status = idle, drone.location = base
      f.archived = true
```

**Client position math (every frame):**
```
elapsed_hr = (now â flight.takeoffAt) / 3600
duration_hr = haversine(origin, dest) / (speedKmh Ă weatherModifierAtTakeoff)
t = clamp(elapsed_hr / duration_hr, 0, 1)
lat = lerp(origin.lat, dest.lat, t)
lng = lerp(origin.lng, dest.lng, t)
battery = batteryAtTakeoff â elapsed_hr Ă DRAIN_PER_HR
```

**Tunable constants:**
- Base speed: 15 km/h
- Weather modifier: clear 1.0 / wind 0.7 / storm 0.0 (grounds)
- Battery drain: base 80 %/hr / storm 120 %/hr
- Storm abort dice: 20% per tick
- Battery-low threshold: 15%
- Mechanical dice: 1% per tick
- Delivering hold: 60s

**`weatherMod(weather, status)`** â returns 1.0 if status is `delivering` (hovering/landing, weather only affects via mechanical dice). Otherwise returns table value.

**`etaAt`** â set at flight creation: `takeoffAt + (distance_km / (speedKmh Ă weatherModifierAtTakeoff)) Ă 3600s`. Re-derived client-side every frame.

**9. Security rules (Firestore):**

```
users/{uid}
  read: self OR admin
  create: self (during register); role forced to "user"
  update: self for profile fields; admin for {role, locked}

catalog/{id}       read auth ; write admin only
drones/{id}        read auth ; write admin only (and Functions on tick)
weather/current    read auth ; write admin only

requests/{id}
  read: owner OR admin
  create: self if userId == auth.uid AND status == "pending"
  update: DENY (must go through callable)

flights/{id}
  read: owner of linked request OR admin
  write: DENY (Functions only)

users/{uid}/notifications/{nid}
  read: self
  write: DENY (Functions only)
```

Rules tested via `@firebase/rules-unit-testing` in `firestore.rules.test.ts` (CI gate).

**10. Callable Cloud Functions (API surface):**

**User-callable:**
- `submitRequest(items[], deliveryAddress, priority?, notes?)`
- `cancelRequest(reqId)` â only while pending
- `confirmDelivery(reqId)`
- `updateProfile(fields)`

**Admin-callable:**
- `approveRequest(reqId)`
- `rejectRequest(reqId, reason)`
- `assignDrone(reqId, droneId)`
- `setWeather(state)`
- `restockItem(itemId, qty)`
- `toggleDroneMaintenance(droneId, on)`
- `createCatalogItem(itemData)`

**Internal / scheduled:**
- `tickFlights` â every 60s via `onSchedule`
- `onFlightWritten` â Firestore trigger, fans out FCM on state transitions
- `onUserCreated` â Auth trigger, provisions `users/{uid}` with role=user

Every callable runs as `https.onCall`, validates `context.auth`, enforces role inside function body.

## SUMMARY

**DroneAid** is a **well-documented, agent-assisted Flutter + Firebase project** with:

1. **Clear problem space:** Simulated drone delivery for civilians impacted by war, with realistic failure modes.
2. **Comprehensive documentation stack:** Concept docs, personas, journey maps, software architecture (C4), implementation diagrams (sequences, state machines, ER), page-by-page design specs with Gherkin AC, full design spec, ADRs, RACI, WBS, GANTT.
3. **5-dev team split with clear ownership:** Aok (backend), Belle (auth + UI kit), Bew (request domain), Poom (tracking + maps), Tawan (fleet). Dependencies explicitly mapped; Belle's widget stubs unblock parallel work (ADR-0003).
4. **14-day deadline (2026-06-04):** 37.5 estimated person-days against 70 available (32.5 buffer); fully mapped day-by-day.
5. **Emulator-first development:** Firebase Emulator Suite for all 5 devs; FCM push-to-device exempt (in-app inbox fallback).
6. **Auditable Claude Code usage:** Every dev's session JSONL committed to `docs/agent-logs/<handle>/` with redaction, CI-enforced daily logs.
7. **GitHub workflow:** 22 issues (#9-#30, milestone "2026-06-04 submission"), standard per-dev branching + PR review.
8. **Well-defined "done":** All promised features on emulator, seeded demo dataset, 16 prototype screens across user + admin portals, all AC passing.

**No CONTEXT.md exists yet** â it's lazily generated by `/grill-with-docs` when domain terms get disambiguated.

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully read all 10 files in full. Here are the complete contents:

---

## File 1: 00-team-raci.md

**Team Assignment & RACI**
- **Project window:** 2026-05-22 â 2026-06-04 (14 days, 7-day work week)
- **Team size:** 5
- **Lead:** Aok

**Domain ownership:**
- **Aok (Lead):** Backend + integration + deploy â Firestore rules, Cloud Functions, sim engine, FCM, seed scripts, CI/CD
- **Belle:** Identity + shared UI kit â Login, register, profile, settings, shared widgets (DroneMap, BatteryBar, StatusChip, ItemPicker)
- **Bew:** Request domain (user + admin) â User-side: Request, Queue, History. Admin-side: Requests list, Request Manage, Supply Inventory
- **Poom:** Tracking + maps (user + admin) â User-side: Tracking, Confirm, Notifications. Admin-side: Control live map
- **Tawan:** Fleet domain (admin) â Admin-side: Drone list, Drone detail, Weather panel

**RACI table:** Detailed ownership matrix across all pages/artifacts with R/A/C/I designations.

**Dependency map:**
- Aok publishes API contract Day 1 â Belle, Bew, Poom, Tawan
- Belle ships shared widgets by Day 4 â Bew, Poom, Tawan
- Aok deploys Cloud Functions progressively Days 3â8 â enables consumers

**Communication:** Daily 15-minute standups, blockers in Discord, 1-reviewer PRs required.

---

## File 2: 01-concepts.md

**Concept & Function List**
- **Date:** 2026-05-19
- **Course:** CSC291, KMUTT
- **Status:** Approved

**Problem statement:** In active conflict zones, civilians need supply delivery without exposing couriers. Drones provide fast, low-risk delivery; coordination requires a mobile app for requesting supplies, vetting by coordinators, and real-time tracking.

**Vision:** Civilians submit relief requests from a catalog, mark delivery pins, admins approve/assign drones, users watch live tracking with battery/ETA/weather visible.

**Goals:**
1. End-to-end delivery flow working under multi-user, real-time conditions
2. Realistic simulation with weather, battery, mechanical failure modes
3. Single mobile codebase serving both user and admin roles
4. Auditable Claude Code usage (session logs committed per professor requirement)
5. Demonstrable on emulator for class presentation

**Non-goals:**
- Real weather API integration, real SMS/OTP auth, government ID verification
- Payment/scoring/rating system, analytics dashboard
- iOS push notifications (Android FCM only)
- Multi-tenancy support

**Personas:**
- Mali Suwan (37, refugee mother) â primary user
- Naree Charoen (29, relief coordinator) â admin user

**Functional list (36 features across 10 categories):**
- F-AUTH-01..07: Registration, login, logout, profile, account locking
- F-CAT-01..05: Catalog management
- F-REQ-01..06: User request lifecycle
- F-ADM-01..06: Admin request management
- F-FLT-01..04: Drone fleet management
- F-SIM-01..08: Delivery tracking & simulation
- F-CFM-01..04: Delivery confirmation
- F-NTF-01..05: Notifications via FCM
- F-SEC-01..05: Security (Firestore rules, callable functions, gitleaks, redaction)
- F-AUD-01..03: Auditability (session logs, CI gate, auto-indexed)

**Tech stack:**
- Mobile: Flutter (Android primary, iOS stretch)
- State: Riverpod
- Routing: go_router
- Map: flutter_map + OpenStreetMap tiles
- Backend: Firebase (Auth, Firestore, Cloud Functions in TypeScript, FCM)
- Scheduled jobs: Cloud Functions onSchedule
- CI/CD: GitHub Actions
- Local dev: Firebase Emulator Suite

**Acceptance criteria:** End-to-end flow from register â submit â approve â assign â track â confirm â return home to idle; storm can abort and trigger reassign; all writes mediated by functions/rules; every dev has session logs.

---

## File 3: 04-journey-map.md

**User Journey Map**

**Persona 1 (User):** Mali Suwan â 37, refugee mother of two, limited cellular signal, smartphone literate.
**Persona 2 (Admin):** Naree Charoen â 29, relief coordinator, manages dispatch from forward base.

**Journey A â Happy path (medical supplies delivered)**

18-stage annotated table + Mermaid diagram tracking Mali's and Naree's emotions (1â5 scale):
1. Mali realizes need (emotion 2)
2. Opens app, logs in (emotion 4)
3. Browses catalog, picks Medical Kit (emotion 4)
4. Drops pin, submits (emotion 4) â `submitRequest` callable
5. Sees "Pending" in Queue (emotion 3)
6. Naree sees alert (emotion 4) â FCM push
7. Naree opens request, reviews user profile + items (emotion 4)
8. Naree approves (emotion 4) â `approveRequest` callable, stock decrements
9. Naree picks DRN-003 from eligible list (emotion 4)
10. Naree assigns â flight created, drone takes off (emotions both 4â5)
11. Mali gets push "Drone dispatched, ETA 18 min" (emotion 5) â `onFlightWritten`
12. Mali watches drone move on map (emotion 4) â client interpolation
13. Mali sees battery 78%, ETA 6 min (emotion 4)
14. Drone arrives, status â Delivering (emotion 4) â `tickFlights` transition
15. Mali hears drone, retrieves supplies (emotion 5)
16. Mali taps Confirm (emotion 5) â `confirmDelivery` callable
17. Drone returns, lands, becomes idle (emotion 5 for Naree)
18. Request appears in History (emotion 5)

**Pain points + mitigations:**
- Wait uncertainty â live status streaming, push on admin open
- Map strain on low-end phones â cap markers, cache tiles
- Confirm easy to miss â persistent banner, push, auto-confirm reminder

**Journey B â Failure + reassign (storm aborts first drone)**

12-stage scenario where Naree sets weather to `storm`, approves anyway (high priority), assigns DRN-005. Mid-flight, `tickFlights` rolls 20% storm dice â ABORT. Mali gets push "Flight aborted, drone returning". Naree gets push "DRN-005 aborted, reassign needed". Naree filters fleet, sets weather to `wind`, assigns DRN-002. Second attempt succeeds. Mali confirms, history shows both flights.

**Pain points + mitigations:**
- User unaware storms ground drones â show weather badge + warning, explain in onboarding
- Reassign requires re-opening Request Manage â surface action from notification
- Two flights confuse users â group under same requestId in history

**Rendering:**
```bash
npx --yes @mermaid-js/mermaid-cli -i docs/04-journey-map.md -o docs/diagrams/04-journey-map.png
```

---

## File 4: 06-wbs.md

**Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)**

**Mermaid mindmap** decomposing DroneAid into 9 top-level branches:
1. **Planning & Documentation** â 10 artifacts (concept, personas, journey, backlog, GANTT, WBS, architecture, implementation, design spec, RACI)
2. **Backend [Aok]** â Firebase setup, rules, models, triggers, 9 callables, tickFlights, onFlightWritten, emulator, seed scripts
3. **Identity + Shared UI [Belle]** â scaffold, login, register, Thai ID validator, profile, settings, 4 shared widgets
4. **Request Domain [Bew]** â catalog, pin picker, submit, queue, history, admin list, manage, drone picker, inventory
5. **Tracking + Maps [Poom]** â tracking page, confirm, notifications, FCM registration, control live map
6. **Fleet Domain [Tawan]** â drone list, drone detail, weather panel, fleet seed data
7. **Quality & Integration** â widget tests, function tests, rules tests, integration day, bug bash
8. **CI/CD & Logs Infra** â ci.yml, build-log-index.yml, gitleaks, log-presence, agent logs setup, CODEOWNERS, PR template
9. **Demo & Delivery** â seed dataset, screenshots, screencast, README, presentation slides, polish

**Linear breakdown (for estimation):**
| WBS | Item | Owner | Est. days |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Planning & Documentation | All | 1.0 |
| 2 | Backend | Aok | 8.0 |
| 3 | Identity + Shared UI | Belle | 5.5 |
| 4 | Request Domain | Bew | 6.0 |
| 5 | Tracking + Maps | Poom | 5.5 |
| 6 | Fleet Domain | Tawan | 4.0 |
| 7 | Quality & Integration | All | 3.0 |
| 8 | CI/CD & Logs Infra | Aok + Belle | 2.5 |
| 9 | Demo & Delivery | All | 2.0 |
| | **Total person-days** | | **37.5** |
| | **Available** (5 devs Ă 14 days) | | **70.0** |
| | **Buffer** (slack, meetings, bug fix) | | **32.5** |

---

## File 5: 07-software-architecture.md

**Software Architecture (C4-style)**

**1. System context (C4 Level 1):**
- External actors: End user (refugee, civilian), Relief coordinator (admin)
- External systems: Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM), OpenStreetMap tiles
- System: DroneAid (simulated drone relief platform)
- Interactions: Users submit requests, watch drones, confirm receipt; admins approve/dispatch/set weather

**2. Containers (C4 Level 2):**
- **Mobile device:** Flutter app (Android, iOS stretch) â Dart + Riverpod
- **Firebase project (csc291-drone-relief):** Firebase Auth, Firestore, Cloud Functions (TypeScript), FCM
- **External:** OpenStreetMap tiles
- **Connections:** Auth ops, realtime streams + callable RPC, FCM token registration, tile fetch (HTTP), on-demand functions, triggers, messaging

**3. Flutter app â internal components (C4 Level 3):**
- **AppRouter (go_router)** â **AuthGate** â User tree | Admin tree
- **User feature tree:** Request, Queue, Tracking, Confirm, History, Notifications, Profile + Settings
- **Admin feature tree:** Control map, Drone list, Drone detail, Requests list, Request Manage, Weather, Inventory
- **Shared widgets:** DroneMap, BatteryBar, StatusChip, ItemPicker
- **Riverpod providers:** authProvider, requestProvider, droneProvider, flightProvider, catalogProvider, weatherProvider
- **Repository layer:** AuthRepo, RequestRepo, DroneRepo, FlightRepo, CatalogRepo, WeatherRepo
- **Utils:** thai_id_validator, geo.dart (haversine, lerp), time_fmt

**Layer responsibilities:**
- **Pages:** UI only; read from providers, call repo methods through providers
- **Providers:** Riverpod state + side effects; streams from repos, debounces, holds form state
- **Repositories:** Single touchpoint to Firebase SDK; returns models (no SDK types leaking)
- **Models:** Pure Dart classes with fromMap/toMap; no SDK imports
- **Shared widgets:** Stateless reusables owned by Belle
- **Utils:** Pure functions (geo math, ID validation, time formatting)

**4. Cloud Functions â internal components:**
- **index.ts** (barrel exports)
- **callable/** (9 functions): submitRequest, approveRequest, rejectRequest, assignDrone, confirmDelivery, cancelRequest, setWeather, restockItem, toggleDroneMaintenance
- **scheduled/tickFlights** (every 60s)
- **triggers:** onUserCreated, onFlightWritten (state-transition FCM)
- **lib:** sim/ (physics, dice), geo.ts, weather.ts, fcm.ts, id.ts, roles.ts (requireAdmin, requireUser)
- **seed/:** seedCatalog, seedDrones, seedAdmins

**5. Cross-cutting concerns:**
- **Auth:** Firebase Auth on wire; context.auth.uid + users/{uid}.role for authorization inside callables
- **Realtime:** Firestore listeners for queue, drone, flight, weather; battery + position derived client-side
- **Concurrency:** Stock decrement, drone assignment use Firestore transactions inside callables
- **Security rules:** DENY direct writes; only Functions write
- **Failure handling:** Callables return HttpsError with codes; client maps to localized messages
- **Testing:** Widget tests with mock repos, Function tests against emulator, rules tests via @firebase/rules-unit-testing
- **Observability:** Cloud Functions logs; Firestore audit collection deferred

---

## File 6: 08-implementation-diagram.md

**Implementation Diagrams**

**1. Deployment view:**
- **Developer machines (Ă5):** Aok (Linux + emulator), Belle/Bew/Poom/Tawan (Windows + Flutter SDK)
- **GitHub:** Repo (main + feat/* branches), GitHub Actions (ci, log-index)
- **GCP/Firebase project (deferred):** Auth, Firestore, Cloud Functions (asia-southeast1), FCM, Cloud Scheduler (60s cron)
- **End-user devices:** Android phone (primary), iOS (stretch)

Flows: Devs â git push â repo â Actions; Cloud Scheduler â tickFlights; Functions â Firestore, Auth, FCM; Phone â Auth/Firestore/Functions/FCM

**2. Sequence â happy-path delivery:**
1. User submits request â Flutter calls submitRequest
2. Backend validates, creates requests/{id} status=pending
3. FCM notifies admins
4. Admin opens Request Manage, sees user profile + items
5. Admin approves â approveRequest, stock decremented, eligible drones returned
6. Admin picks drone, assigns â assignDrone creates flight enroute, drone flying
7. FCM to user: "Dispatched"
8. Scheduled tick (60s): read weather, query flights, compute progress + battery, roll dice
9. Progress reaches 1.0 â status delivering
10. Next tick â status completed, request delivered, FCM "confirm please"
11. User confirms â confirmDelivery, request confirmed, flight returning
12. tickFlights brings drone home â idle

**3. Sequence â storm aborts, admin reassigns:**
1. Weather set to storm (earlier)
2. User submits, admin approves and assigns DRN-005
3. tickFlights every 60s: reads weather=storm, rolls rand() < 0.20 â ABORT
4. Flight status aborted, request failed, drone idle
5. FCM to user + admins
6. Admin re-enters Request Manage
7. Admin sets weather to wind
8. Admin assigns DRN-002 (2nd flight)
9. tickFlights advances to delivering â completed
10. FCM notifies user
11. User confirms

**4. Request status state machine:**
```
[*] â pending â approved â in_flight â delivered â confirmed â [*]
       â          â            â           
       rejected   failed â aborted/mechanical
       â          â
       [*]        â in_flight (reassign)
       cancelled â [*]
```

**5. Flight status state machine:**
```
[*] â enroute â delivering â completed â returning â [*]
       â                                  
       aborted ââââââââââââââââââââââââââ [*]
```

**6. Drone status state machine:**
```
[*] â idle â flying â idle / maintenance
       â     â
       maintenance â idle
       offline â idle
```

**7. Entity-relationship (Firestore collections):**
- USER (uid, nationalId, name, phone, role, deliveryAddress, locked)
  - submits â REQUEST
  - receives â NOTIFICATION
  - has pin â ADDRESS
- REQUEST (reqId, userId, items, totalWeightKg, status, priority, currentFlightId, createdAt)
  - contains â CATALOG_ITEM (1-to-many)
  - spawns â FLIGHT (1-to-many)
- DRONE (droneId, status, batteryPct, baseLocation, maxPayloadKg, currentFlightId)
  - assigned â FLIGHT
- FLIGHT (flightId, droneId, requestId, status, origin, destination, takeoffAt, etaAt, speedKmh, weatherModifierAtTakeoff, batteryAtTakeoff, failureType)
  - snapshot â WEATHER
- CATALOG_ITEM (itemId, name, weightKg, stock, active)
- NOTIFICATION (nid, type, title, body, requestId, flightId, readAt, createdAt)
- WEATHER (state, updatedAt, updatedBy)

---

## File 7: 09-page-flow-design.md

**Full Page & Flow Design**

**Read order:** Â§1 conventions â Â§2 components â Â§3 navigation â remaining sections.

**1. Conventions:**
- **Identifier system:** P-U-NN (user page), P-A-NN (admin page), C-NN (component), F-NN (flow), E-NN (error), V-NN (validation)
- **Visual design tokens:**
  - Theme: Material 3, seed color #006A6A (teal), light/dark modes via ColorScheme.fromSeed()
  - Typography: Material 3 (heading: headlineSmall 24/32, body: bodyLarge 16/24, label: labelLarge 14/20)
  - Spacing: xs=4, sm=8, md=16, lg=24, xl=32, 2xl=48 (multiples of 4)
  - Shapes: Cards (RoundedRectangleBorder radius:12), M3 buttons, circular map markers (32Ă32, 2px white border)
  - Motion: Shared-axis page transitions, 200ms AnimatedSwitcher, 60fps drone marker animation
- **Copy style:** English only v1, clear/calm/factual tone, date format MMM d h:mm a, distance in km (1 decimal), ETA relative + absolute, all strings keyed in app_en.arb
- **Loading/empty/error pattern:** loading (spinner), empty (illustration + heading + optional CTA), error (icon + "Something went wrong" + retry), data (real content)
- **Navigation rules:** go_router declarative routes; auth gate to /login; role gate to shell; back button on every non-root page; deep links from notifications use ?from=notification query param

**2. Component library (C-NN):**
| ID | Component | Purpose | Key props |
|---|---|---|---|
| C-01 | AppScaffold | Page chrome (AppBar, body, optional bottom-nav) | title, actions, body, showBottomNav |
| C-02 | StatusChip | Color-coded status pill | status: RequestStatus \| FlightStatus \| DroneStatus |
| C-03 | BatteryBar | Horizontal battery gauge | percent: 0..100, lowThreshold |
| C-04 | DroneMap | flutter_map wrapper + drone marker + path | origin, destination, flightPlan, mode |
| C-05 | ItemPickerSheet | Bottom-sheet to add item + qty | items, onAdd |
| C-06 | RequestCard | Compact request card | request, onTap, showUser |
| C-07 | DroneCard | Compact drone card | drone, onTap |
| C-08 | NotificationListTile | Inbox row | notification, onTap |
| C-09 | EmptyState | Empty-state illustration + copy + CTA | icon, title, body, actionLabel, onAction |
| C-10 | ErrorState | Error illustration + copy + retry | error, onRetry |
| C-11 | WeightBar | Live weight vs drone-max progress | currentKg, maxKg=6.0 |
| C-12 | ConfirmDialog | Standard destructive-action dialog | title, body, confirmLabel, dangerous |
| C-13 | MapPinPicker | Fullscreen map to drop/drag pin | initial, onPick |
| C-14 | EtaTicker | Self-updating ETA/battery readout | flight (refreshes every 1s) |
| C-15 | WeatherBadge | Tiny pill showing global weather state | state (admin only opens panel on tap) |
| C-16 | RolePill | "USER" / "ADMIN" badge | role |
| C-17 | LoadingButton | Filled button showing spinner while awaiting | onPressed, loading |
| C-18 | NumericKeypadField | 13-digit national ID input | controller, onComplete |

**3. Navigation map:**
```
/ â AuthGate
  âââ /login (P-U-01)
```

**4. Demo seed dataset:**

**6. Admin pages (P-A-NN):**

**P-A-01 â Requests list:**

**7. Flows (F-NN):**

**F-01 Register:** Form validation â createUserWithEmailAndPassword â onUserCreated trigger provisions users/{uid} â read role â redirect
**F-02 Login:** signInWithEmailAndPassword â read role â redirect to shell
**F-03 Logout:** Confirm â delete FCM token â signOut â /login
**F-04 Edit profile field:** Inline edit â updateProfile callable â "Saved" feedback
**F-05 Set delivery pin:** MapPinPicker â updateProfile callable
**F-06 Add to cart:** ItemPickerSheet â select qty â cartProvider gains row
**F-07 Submit request:** Client builds payload â submitRequest callable â validation (items exist, active, stock â„ qty, weight â€ 6.0, role=user) â create requests/{id} pending â FCM admins â clear cart â /user/queue
**F-08 Watch queue:** Stream requests where userId == me, render by status
**F-09 Cancel pending:** ConfirmDialog â cancelRequest â status=cancelled
**F-10 View tracking:** Subscribe flights/{flightId}, compute live position + battery; sub-flows F-10a state transition, F-10b abort banner
**F-11 Tap notification:** Mark read â navigate per type (request_approved â /user/queue, flight_dispatched â /user/tracking/:flightId, etc.)
**F-12 Receive FCM push:** OS notification â tap â navigate (F-11); if app foreground, in-app banner
**F-13 Confirm delivery:** confirmDelivery callable â request.status=confirmed, flight.status=returning â FCM â snackbar + /user/queue
**F-14 Admin sees new request push:** Push â tap â /admin/requests/:reqId
**F-15 Admin opens request:** Subscribe request + user profile + (when approved) eligibleDronesProvider
**F-16 Admin approves:** approveRequest callable â tx (stock--, status=approved) â return eligible drones â reveal picker
**F-17 Admin rejects:** Reason dialog â rejectRequest callable â status=rejected â FCM user
**F-18 Admin assigns drone:** assignDrone callable â tx (create flight enroute, drone.status=flying, request.status=in_flight) â FCM user "Dispatched"
**F-19 Scheduled tick:** Cloud Scheduler every 60s â tickFlights â read weather, query active flights, compute progress + battery, roll dice, write transitions, FCM
**F-20 Flight aborts:** Dice roll inside F-19 â failureType set, status â aborted, request.status â failed, drone â idle|maintenance â FCM user + admins
**F-21 Admin reassigns after failure:** Request status=failed â banner "Previous attempt failed" â Reassign button â picker â assignDrone (2nd flight)
**F-22 Admin sets weather:** setWeather callable â weather/current state â next tick uses new state
**F-23 Admin restocks item:** Quantity dialog â restockItem callable â stock += qty
**F-24 Admin creates catalog item:** Dialog (name, weight, initial stock, icon) â createCatalogItem callable
**F-25 Admin toggles drone maintenance/offline:** toggleDroneMaintenance callable (disallowed if flying)
**F-26 Admin browses control map:** Subscribe activeFlightsProvider, render markers, tap â bottom sheet
**F-27 FCM device registration:** App start post-login â request permission â get token â arrayUnion users/{uid}.fcmTokens; logout â arrayRemove
**F-28 Theme switch:** User picks Theme â themeProvider updates â MaterialApp rebuild â preference persisted
**F-29 Deep-link from notification (cold start):** OS launches with data.deepLink â AuthGate checks login â if authed, navigate to deepLink; else stash, /login, replay after
**F-30 Account locked mid-session:** users/{uid} listener sees locked=true â sign out + dialog E-03

**8. Errors (E-NN):**

| ID | Trigger | UI treatment | Copy |
|---|---|---|---|
| E-01 | Wrong creds | Snackbar | "Wrong national ID or password." |
| E-02 | Checksum invalid | Inline field error | "Invalid national ID." |
| E-03 | Account locked | Modal dialog | "This account has been locked. Contact your coordinator." |
| E-04 | Network failure | Snackbar + Retry | "Can't reach DroneAid. Check your connection." |
| E-05 | ID already registered | Inline field error | "This national ID is already registered. Log in instead." |
| E-06 | Weak password | Inline field error | "Password must be at least 8 characters..." |
| E-07 | Unknown auth error | Snackbar | "Something went wrong. Please try again." |
| E-08 | Invalid profile field | Inline field error | "Please enter a valid value." |
| E-09 | Item out of stock | Disabled [+] + helper | "Out of stock." |
| E-10 | Cart weight exceeds | Helper + disabled Submit | "Total exceeds drone payload (6.0 kg)." |
| E-11 | Stock changed | Snackbar + reload | "Stock changed: <item> is now <count>." |
| E-12 | Cancel after approval | Snackbar | "Too late â your request has been approved." |
| E-13 | Confirm when not delivered | Banner on Confirm page | "Drone hasn't arrived yet." |
| E-14 | Drone no longer eligible | Snackbar + refresh picker | "DRN-XXX is no longer available..." |
| E-15 | FCM permission denied | Banner on first run | "Push notifications are off. Turn on in Settings..." |
| E-16 | Map tiles fail | Map area + retry | "Map failed to load. Tap to retry." |
| E-17 | Cloud Function timeout | Snackbar | "DroneAid is slow right now. Please try again." |
| E-18 | Stuck flight (>2Ă ETA) | Auto-marks failed mechanical | (server-side; user notified per F-20) |
| E-19 | User confirms twice | Server idempotent | "Already confirmed." |
| E-20 | Assign on non-idle drone | Server rejects | "DRN-XXX changed state. Refresh and pick another." |

**9. Validation rules (V-NN):**

| ID | Field | Rule |
|---|---|---|
| V-01 | National ID | 13 digits, all numeric; Thai checksum: sum(d[i] Ă (13-i) for i in 0..11) % 11 â (11 - r) % 10 == d[12] |
| V-02 | Password | length â„ 8, â„1 letter + â„1 digit |
| V-03 | Full name | length 1â60, non-blank |
| V-04 | Phone | E.164 format, length 10â15 incl. + |
| V-05 | Confirm password | equals password |
| V-06 | Terms checkbox | true |
| V-07 | Cart total weight | > 0 and â€ 6.0 kg |
| V-08 | Delivery pin | lat â [-90,90], lng â [-180,180], not (0,0) |
| V-09 | Request items | length â„ 1, every catalogId exists & active, qty â„ 1 |
| V-10 | Reject reason | non-empty, length â€ 200 |
| V-11 | Restock qty | integer â„ 1, â€ 999 |
| V-12 | Catalog item name | unique among active, length 1â40 |
| V-13 | Drone payload at assign | request.totalWeightKg â€ drone.maxPayloadKg |
| V-14 | Drone range at assign | haversine(drone.baseLocation, destination) â€ 15 km |
| V-15 | Drone battery at assign | drone.batteryPct â„ 30 (warn 30â50) |
| V-16 | Notification deep link | safe internal route only (no external URLs) |

**10. Internationalization keys (English defaults):** All strings in `app/lib/l10n/app_en.arb`; naming convention `screen.section.key`. Sample keys shown for login, home, queue, tracking, confirm, admin screens, weather, inventory.

**11. Accessibility checklist:**
- Every interactive element has `Semantics(label: ...)`
- Minimum tap target 48Ă48 px
- Color contrast â„4.5:1 (body), â„3:1 (large)
- Status indicators use icon + color + text (never color alone)
- TextField labels visible (not placeholder-only)
- Map markers have semantic labels
- Reduced-motion preference disables marker tween, falls back to teleport-on-tick

**12. Performance budgets:**
| Metric | Target |
|---|---|
| Cold start to /login | â€2.5 s (mid-range Android) |
| Login â /user/home | â€1.5 s |
| /user/home first paint (cached catalog) | â€300 ms |
| Tracking marker animation | 60 fps |
| Submit request â server ack | â€1.5 s (Wi-Fi) |
| tickFlights per-tick (â€30 flights) | â€2 s |

**13. Open questions:**
- Auto-confirm after 24h if user never taps Confirm? (Default: no)
- Reassign requires admin note? (Default: no, frictionless)
- Audit log of approve/reject/assign? (Out of v1)
- Photo proof on Confirm page? (Out of v1)

## File 10: 2026-05-19-drone-relief-design.md

**Drone Relief Delivery â Design Spec**
- **Date:** 2026-05-19
- **Project:** CSC291 group project, KMUTT
- **Team size:** 5 developers
- **Timeline:** 2 weeks (10 working days)
- **Status:** Approved by user, pending team review

**Â§1. Problem & Goal:**
Build a mobile application simulating drone-based delivery of relief supplies to civilians impacted by war. Two role-switched portals (user + admin) in single Flutter codebase. Drone fleet fully simulated; realistic end-to-end flow under multi-user, real-time conditions; failure modes drive admin reassignment.

**Goals:**
- Realistic delivery flow
- Demonstrable failures (weather/battery/mechanical)
- Server-of-truth state in Firestore
- Auditable Claude Code usage per professor requirement

**Non-goals:**
- Real weather API, real SMS/OTP, real national ID verification
- Payment/fee/scoring system, analytics, audit log
- iOS push (Android FCM only; iOS stretch)
- KYC integration

**Â§2. Tech Stack:**
| Layer | Choice |
|---|---|
| Mobile client | Flutter (Android primary, iOS stretch) |
| State | Riverpod |
| Routing | go_router |
| Map | flutter_map + OpenStreetMap tiles (no API key) |
| Auth | Firebase Auth (email/password, synthetic email <id>@drone-aid.local) |
| Database | Firestore (realtime) |
| Backend | Firebase Cloud Functions (TypeScript) |
| Push | Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) |
| Scheduled | Cloud Functions onSchedule (every 60s) |
| Local dev | Firebase Emulator Suite (Auth + Firestore + Functions) |
| CI/CD | GitHub Actions |

Why not separate Hono/Elysia: Cloud Functions covers callable RPC + scheduled cron + triggers natively; 2-week scope can't absorb Docker + Cloud Run + WebSocket plumbing. If team later wants Hono, callable can be ported without changing data model.

**Â§3. Architecture:**
```
Flutter app (single APK/IPA)
  USER screens | ADMIN screens | SHARED
       âââââ Repository layer âââââ
              Riverpod providers
       Firebase SDK (Auth/Firestore/FCM/Functions)
                    âŒ
  ââââââââââââââ FIREBASE ââââââââââââââ
   Auth   Firestore   Cloud Functions     FCM
                      (9 callables, 1 scheduled, 2 triggers)
```

**Key decisions:**
- One Flutter codebase, role-switched via users/{uid}.role
- Repository layer wraps Firestore; screens never touch SDK (allows mocks)
- Drone position computed client-side from flightPlan (origin, destination, takeoffAt, speedKmh, weatherModifier) â server only writes transitions (eliminates per-second writes, keeps free tier safe)
- State-changing actions go through callable Cloud Functions (no direct Firestore writes; centralizes logic, atomic transactions)

**Â§4. Data Model (Firestore):**

Collections:
- **users/{uid}** â nationalId (unique), name, phone, role (user|admin), deliveryAddress {lat, lng, label}, locked, fcmTokens [], createdAt
- **users/{uid}/notifications/{nid}** â type, title, body, requestId?, flightId?, readAt, createdAt
- **catalog/{itemId}** â name, weightKg, stock, icon, active
- **requests/{reqId}** â userId, items [{catalogId, qty}], totalWeightKg, deliveryAddress, priority (normal|urgent), status (pending|approved|rejected|in_flight|delivered|confirmed|failed|cancelled), notes, decidedBy, decidedAt, rejectReason, currentFlightId, createdAt
- **drones/{droneId}** â name, status (idle|flying|charging|maintenance|offline), batteryPct, baseLocation {lat, lng}, maxPayloadKg, currentFlightId?, lastSeenAt
- **flights/{flightId}** â droneId, requestId, userId, status (enroute|delivering|returning|completed|aborted|failed), origin, destination, takeoffAt, etaAt, speedKmh, weatherModifierAtTakeoff, batteryAtTakeoff, failureType, tickHistory [{ts, lat, lng, batteryPct, event}]
- **weather/current** â state (clear|wind|storm), updatedBy, updatedAt

**Modeling notes:**
- Flight separate from request (request = "what user wants", flight = "delivery attempt"; reassigns spawn new flights)
- Snapshot address on request (retains target even if user moves pin)
- Stock lives on catalog, decremented atomically inside approveRequest
- Position derived, not stored (saves ~3,600 writes/hr per drone)

**Â§5. Auth, Roles, Identity:**
- Registration collects: national ID (13-digit + checksum validated), password, name, phone, optional delivery pin
- Firebase Auth uses synthetic email <id>@drone-aid.local
- On first sign-in, onUserCreated trigger provisions users/{uid} with role:user
- Admin accounts seeded via seedAdmins.ts script (one-time, idempotent)
- ID checksum logic in app/lib/utils/thai_id_validator.dart AND functions/src/lib/id.ts; both tested

**Â§6. Pages:**

**User portal (8 screens):**
1. Login / Register
2. Home / Request (catalog, cart, pin, submit)
3. Queue (own requests, live status)
4. Tracking (live map, interpolated position)
5. Confirm (finalize delivery)
6. History (completed / failed / cancelled)
7. Notifications (in-app inbox)
8. Profile + Settings (edit, theme, logout)

**Admin portal (7 screens):**
1. Control (live map of all flights)
2. Drone list (all drones, filters)
3. Drone detail (status, battery, history)
4. Requests (list, filters by status/priority)
5. Request manage (open one, approve/reject, assign drone, eligible picker)
6. Weather panel (set global clear/wind/storm)
7. Supply inventory (restock, toggle active, add new)

**Â§7. End-to-end flow:**
```
USER                          BACKEND                          ADMIN
[Request page]
  submit ââââââââââ¶  submitRequest()
                       validate items + stock + weight â€ max payload
                       create requests/{id} status=pending
                       FCM ping admins ââââââââââââââââ¶  [Request list]

                                                            [Request manage]
                                                              approve / reject

                       approveRequest({reqId})  âââââââ
                         tx: stock--, status=approved
                         return eligible drones

                                                              pick drone
                       assignDrone({reqId, droneId})  âââââââ
                         tx: create flights/{id} enroute
                             takeoffAt=now()
                             etaAt = now + distance / (speedKmh Ă weatherMod)
                         drone.status=flying, drone.currentFlightId=flightId
                         request.status=in_flight, request.currentFlightId=flightId
                         FCM ping user ââââââ¶
[Tracking page]                                             [Control map]
  drone moves live                                            drone visible

       âââââ tickFlights (every 60s) âââââ¶
         roll failures, advance status

       (no failure)
         delivering â completed
         request.status=delivered
         FCM ping user ââââââ¶
[Confirm page]
  â confirm âââââââ¶  confirmDelivery({reqId})
                       request.status=confirmed
                       drone.status=returning â tick brings home â idle
```

Failure branch:
```
tick rolls failureType â {weather, battery, mechanical}
  flight.status = aborted or failed
  request.status = failed
  drone.status = idle (battery > 0) | maintenance
  FCM ping user + admin
```

**Â§8. Drone simulator engine:**

**Scheduled tick (every 60s):**
```
tickFlights():
  weather = read weather/current
  flights = query flights where status in [enroute, delivering, returning]

  for f in flights:
    elapsed_hr      = (now - f.takeoffAt) / 3600
    distance_km     = haversine(f.origin, f.destination)
    effective_speed = f.speedKmh Ă weatherMod(weather, f.status)
    progress        = clamp(elapsed_hr Ă effective_speed / distance_km, 0, 1)
    battery         = f.batteryAtTakeoff â elapsed_hr Ă DRAIN_PER_HR(weather)

    if f.status == enroute:
      if weather == storm and rand() < 0.20: â abort("weather")
      if battery < 15                      : â abort("battery")
      if rand() < 0.01                     : â abort("mechanical")

    if aborted:
      f.failureType, f.status = aborted | failed
      request.status = failed
      drone.status = idle | maintenance
      notify(user, admin)

    elif progress >= 1.0 and f.status == enroute:
      f.status = delivering          # 60s soft hold for "landing"
      notify(user, "drone arriving")

    elif progress >= 1.0 and f.status == delivering:
      f.status = completed
      request.status = delivered
      notify(user, "please confirm")

    elif progress >= 1.0 and f.status == returning:
      drone.status = idle, drone.location = base
      f.archived = true
```

**Client position math (every animation frame):**
```
elapsed_hr  = (now â flight.takeoffAt) / 3600
duration_hr = haversine(origin, dest) / (speedKmh Ă weatherModifierAtTakeoff)
t = clamp(elapsed_hr / duration_hr, 0, 1)
lat = lerp(origin.lat, dest.lat, t)
lng = lerp(origin.lng, dest.lng, t)
battery = batteryAtTakeoff â elapsed_hr Ă DRAIN_PER_HR
```

**Tunable constants:**
| Constant | Default |
|---|---|
| Base speed | 15 km/h |
| Weather mod (clear/wind/storm) | 1.0 / 0.7 / 0.0 |
| Battery drain (base/storm) | 80 / 120 %/hr |
| Storm abort dice per tick | 20% |
| Battery-low threshold | 15% |
| Mechanical dice per tick | 1% |
| Delivering hold | 60s |

**weatherMod(weather, status):** Returns 1.0 if status==delivering (hovering/landing, weather only affects via mechanical dice). Otherwise table value. Returning uses enroute modifier.

**etaAt:** Set at flight creation: takeoffAt + (distance_km / (speedKmh Ă weatherModifierAtTakeoff)) Ă 3600s. Re-derived client-side every frame.

**Â§9. Security rules (Firestore):**
```
users/{uid}
  read    : self OR admin
  create  : self (during register); role forced to "user"
  update  : self for profile fields; admin for {role, locked}

catalog/{id}      read auth ; write admin only
drones/{id}       read auth ; write admin only (and Functions on tick)
weather/current   read auth ; write admin only

requests/{id}
  read    : owner OR admin
  create  : self if userId == auth.uid AND status == "pending"
  update  : DENY (must go through callable)

flights/{id}
  read    : owner of linked request OR admin
  write   : DENY (Functions only)

users/{uid}/notifications/{nid}
  read    : self
  write   : DENY (Functions only)
```

Rules tested via @firebase/rules-unit-testing in firestore.rules.test.ts (CI gate).

**Â§10. Callable Cloud Functions (the API surface):**

**User-callable:**
- submitRequest(items[], deliveryAddress, priority?, notes?)
- cancelRequest(reqId) â only while pending
- confirmDelivery(reqId)
- updateProfile(fields)

**Admin-callable:**
- approveRequest(reqId)
- rejectRequest(reqId, reason)
- assignDrone(reqId, droneId)
- setWeather(state)
- restockItem(itemId, qty)
- toggleDroneMaintenance(droneId, on)
- createCatalogItem(itemData)

**Internal / scheduled:**
- tickFlights â every 60s via onSchedule
- onFlightWritten â Firestore trigger, fans FCM on state transitions
- onUserCreated â Auth trigger, provisions users/{uid} with role=user

Every callable runs as https.onCall, validates context.auth, enforces role inside function body.

**Â§11. Repository layout:**

**Â§12. Team split & 2-week timeline:**

**Owners:**
| Dev | Owns |
|---|---|
| A (Aok) | Auth + register + profile + settings + shared core (router, theme, ID validator) |
| B (Belle) | User request flow: catalog, cart, pin, submit, queue, history |
| C (Poom) | User tracking + confirm + notifications + FCM client + DroneMap widget |
| D (Tawan) | All admin pages: control, drone list, drone detail, requests, manage, weather, inventory |
| E (Bew) | Backend: Firestore rules, seed, all Cloud Functions, sim, FCM, deploy |

Actually, wait â the spec lists differently. Let me correct:

| Dev | Owns |
|---|---|
| A (Aok) | **Backend:** All Cloud Functions, sim engine, Firestore rules, seed scripts, FCM fan-out, CI/CD |
| B (Belle) | **Identity + shared UI kit:** Login, register, profile, settings, DroneMap, BatteryBar, StatusChip, ItemPicker, Thai ID validator, theme, router scaffold |
| C (Bew) | **Request domain:** User Request, Queue, History; Admin Requests list, Request Manage, Drone picker, Inventory |
| D (Poom) | **Tracking + maps:** User Tracking, Confirm, Notifications; Admin Control live map, FCM device registration |
| E (Tawan) | **Fleet domain:** Admin Drone list, Drone detail, Weather panel, seed drone data |

**Day-by-day:**

**Week 1 â foundation + happy path:**
- Day 1: Aok Firebase + rules v0 + models + seed. Belle Flutter scaffold + theme + go_router + Riverpod. All: emulator.
- Day 2: Belle login + register + ID validator (+ tests). Aok onUserCreated. Bew/Poom/Tawan model + repo stubs.
- Day 3: Bew catalog + cart. Tawan admin requests list. Aok submitRequest + tests.
- Day 4: Bew submit + queue (live). Tawan request manage approve/reject. Aok approveRequest + rejectRequest + stock tx.
- Day 5: Poom tracking + interpolation. Tawan drone list + detail. Aok assignDrone + flight + tickFlights v0 (movement).

**Week 2 â failures, polish, demo:**
- Day 6: Poom confirm + confirmDelivery. Tawan control live map. Aok state transitions (delivered â completed).
- Day 7: Aok failure dice. Tawan weather panel. Bew history.
- Day 8: Belle/Poom FCM device setup + notifications inbox. Aok onFlightWritten â FCM. Tawan inventory.
- Day 9: All integration testing on emulator + real Firebase dev project. Bug bash.
- Day 10: All demo data seed, polish, screenshots, video, README.

**Risks & mitigations:**
| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| Firestore rules deny silently | Emulator Day 1, rules unit tests in CI |
| First tick cold-start lag | Min instances = 1 on tickFlights, or accept first-90s delay |
| Map perf with many markers | Cap demo fleet at 8â12 drones |
| Thai ID checksum bugs | Validator + tests Day 2 |
| iOS push needs APNs cert | Android FCM only; iOS push stretch |
| 5-dev integration drift | Daily 15-min sync + shared seed dataset |

**Â§13. Claude Code agent-log storage:**

**Goal:** Every team member's Claude Code session logs. Centralize in repo so professor can audit via git clone.

**Layout:**
```
docs/agent-logs/

```

**Per-dev setup (one-time):**
1. Clone repo, pick dev handle, create folder docs/agent-logs/<handle>/.
2. Add SessionEnd hook to ~/.claude/settings.json:
   ```json
   {
     "hooks": {
       "SessionEnd": [{
         "matcher": "",
         "hooks": [{
           "type": "command",
           "command": "pwsh -File <repo>/scripts/copy-claude-session.ps1"
         }]
       }]
     }
   }
   ```
   (mac/linux uses scripts/copy-claude-session.sh.)
3. Script copies ~/.claude/projects/D--projects-csc291/<session>.jsonl to docs/agent-logs/<handle>/<date>_<session>.jsonl, piping through redact-secrets.py.
4. End of day: git add + commit.

**Redaction (scripts/redact-secrets.py):**
Strips before write: Google API keys, OpenAI/Anthropic-shaped keys, OAuth tokens, Firebase service account JSON, Bearer tokens, email + 13-digit Thai ID â [REDACTED]

**Repo hygiene:**
- .gitattributes: docs/agent-logs/**/*.jsonl -diff linguist-vendored=true (clean diffs, mark vendored on GitHub)
- _index.md regenerated by scripts/build-log-index.py on push (see Â§15)
- Daily commit rule (not per-session) â keeps history clean

**Â§14. Project subagents (.claude/agents/):**

Committed to repo. Whole team uses same definitions.

| Agent | Role | Tools | Model |
|---|---|---|---|
| architect | Design, schema change, API shape before code | Read, Grep, Glob, WebFetch | opus |
| engineer | Implement approved design: Flutter pages, Cloud Functions, refactors | Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash | sonnet |
| qa | Unit + widget + integration tests, emulator runs, rules tests | Read, Edit, Write, Grep, Glob, Bash | sonnet |
| security-reviewer | Pre-merge audit: rules holes, auth bypass, secrets, input validation, callable auth | Read, Grep, Glob, Bash | opus |

Each agent file (.claude/agents/<name>.md) holds frontmatter (name, description, tools, model) + system prompt (role, refusals, output format).

**Â§15. GitHub workflows (.github/workflows/):**

**CI (ci.yml) â runs on PR and push:**
| Job | Purpose |
|---|---|
| flutter-test | flutter analyze + flutter test in app/; pub cache enabled |
| functions-test | npm ci + npm run lint + npm test in functions/ |
| rules-test | Firestore emulator + @firebase/rules-unit-testing against firestore.rules |
| secrets-scan | gitleaks on diff; fails if key pattern slipped |
| log-presence | PR must add â„1 new *.jsonl under docs/agent-logs/<author-handle>/ if any source changed. Author handle from docs/agent-logs/README.md GitHub-username table. Enforces class rule. |

**Deploy:**
~~deploy-functions.yml~~ + ~~deploy-rules.yml~~ â **deferred, emulator-only**. Class submission runs against Firebase Emulator Suite on each dev's laptop. To re-enable auto-deploy later, recreate from git history + add FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT secret. See docs/adr/0002-scope-full-features-fcm-emulator-exempt.md.

**Housekeeping:**
- build-log-index.yml: on push to docs/agent-logs/**: regenerate _index.md, commit back with [skip ci]
- claude-review.yml *(optional, can drop)* â on PR, invoke security-reviewer subagent against diff, post findings as comment

**Repo settings:**
- Single Firebase project droneaid-csc291 in .firebaserc, not deployed for class â local emulator only
- ~~FIREBASE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT in secret~~ deferred until deploy re-enabled
- main branch protected: require CI green + 1 reviewer
- Branch convention: feat/<short>, PR â main

**Â§16. Acceptance criteria (definition of done):**

- User can register with Thai national ID + password, log in, reach Request page
- User can submit request with 1+ items â€ drone payload
- Admin sees new request live, approves, picks eligible drone
- User sees drone moving on Tracking with live battery + ETA
- Storm weather can abort in-flight drone; user + admin both notified
- Delivered request triggers Confirm page; confirmation moves to History
- Drone returns to idle after success/failure
- All Firestore writes mediated by Functions/rules; direct writes denied
- CI passes: flutter, functions, rules, gitleaks, log-presence tests
- Every dev has â„1 session log per day they committed code
- Demo dataset seeds: 8 drones, 6 items, 1 admin, 3 demo users

**Â§17. Open questions (resolve during implementation):**

- Localization: Thai-only, English-only, or both? (Default: Thai + English fallback)
- Demo geographic region? (Default: Bangkok metro, 15 km radius)
- Auto-confirm if user doesn't tap within N hours? (Default: no)
- Drone re-charge time after return? (Default: instantaneous; can add 5-min cooldown if time permits)

---

**Summary of all 10 files provided:** Complete specification of a Flutter + Firebase drone relief delivery simulator with 5-person team ownership across backend, shared UI, user features (request/tracking/notifications), admin features (drones/requests/live map), and comprehensive design documentation including personas, journey maps, architecture, implementation diagrams, page flows with Gherkin acceptance criteria, error handling, validation rules, component library, navigation maps, demo seed data, and detailed day-by-day development timeline.

input.content:
# Project Context: CSC291-DroneAid â Full Documentation Review

## Why this exists
Pre-implementation orientation. All project .md files, docs/, and backend schema reviewed before writing any code.
No Prisma schema exists â data models live in TypeScript callables + Firestore rules.

---

## Project at a glance

**DroneAid** â drone-based relief-supply delivery simulator. Flutter + Firebase mobile app. War-zone civilians request supplies, admins approve + assign drones, users track live. CSC291 KMUTT group project.

| | |
|---|---|
| Deadline | 2026-06-04 (submission 2026-06-05) |
| Scope | Emulator-only â no real Firebase deploy (ADR-0002) |
| Platform | Android only (iOS/web disabled) |
| Dev env | Native Windows (ADR-0001) |
| Dev command | `bun scripts/dev.ts` (build + emulators + seed + tsc-watch + Flutter) |

---

## Team ownership (RACI)

| Person | GitHub | Domain |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Aok (Lead) | @AokDesu | Backend: Firestore rules, Cloud Functions, sim engine, FCM, seed, CI/CD |
| Belle | @AokDesu(?) | Identity + shared UI: Login, Register, Profile, Settings, shared widgets |
| Bew | @SNOWxSAUSAGES | Request domain: P-U-03..04, P-U-07, P-A-01, P-A-02, P-A-07 |
| **Poom** | **@Mrmo0p** | **Tracking + maps: P-U-05, P-U-06, P-U-08, P-A-05, #28 spike** |
| Tawan | @Tantawan7 | Fleet domain: P-A-03, P-A-04, P-A-06 |

Belle ships shared widgets by **Day 4 (2026-05-25)** â consumers (Bew, Poom, Tawan) unblocked after that (ADR-0003).

---

## Tech stack

| Layer | Choice |
|-------|--------|
| Flutter | 3.22+, Dart 3.4+ |
| State | Riverpod (StreamProvider, FutureProvider) |
| Routing | go_router (role-aware redirect guard) |
| Maps | flutter_map 7.0.2 + OpenStreetMap tiles + latlong2 |
| Auth | Firebase Auth (synthetic email: `{nationalId}@drone-aid.local`) |
| DB | Cloud Firestore |
| Functions | Firebase Functions v6 TypeScript, Node ES2022, asia-southeast1 |
| Validation | Zod (function payloads) |
| Messaging | FCM (in-app inbox under emulator; push-to-device exempt per ADR-0002) |
| Dev runner | Bun + `scripts/dev.ts` |
| Tests | Jest (functions), no Flutter tests yet |

---

## Data model (Firestore â no Prisma)

### `users/{uid}`
`nationalId, name, phone, role("admin"|"user"), deliveryAddress{lat,lng,label}, locked, fcmTokens[], createdAt, lastActivityAt`
Sub: `notifications/{nid}`

### `catalog/{itemId}`
`name, weightKg, stock, icon, active`

### `requests/{reqId}`
`userId, items[{catalogId,qty}], totalWeightKg, deliveryAddress{lat,lng,label}, priority("normal"|"urgent"), notes, status("pending"â"approved"â"in_flight"â"delivered"â"confirmed"|"rejected"|"failed"|"cancelled"), decidedBy, decidedAt, rejectReason, currentFlightId`

### `drones/{droneId}`
`name, baseLocation{lat,lng}, maxPayloadKg, batteryPct, status("idle"|"flying"|"maintenance"|"offline"), currentFlightId`

### `flights/{flightId}`
`droneId, requestId, userId, origin{lat,lng}, destination{lat,lng}, takeoffAt, etaAt, speedKmh, weatherModifierAtTakeoff, batteryAtTakeoff, status("enroute"â"delivering"â"completed"|"returning"âidle), failureType("weather"|"battery"|"mechanical"), archived, deliveringStartedAt, returningStartedAt`

### `weather/current`
`state("clear"|"rain"|"storm")` â speed modifier: clear 1.0Ă, rain 0.85Ă, storm 0.6Ă

---

## Cloud Functions (13 total)

### Callables
| Function | Who | Purpose |
|----------|-----|---------|
| `submitRequest` | User | Create pending request; validate â€6kg + stock |
| `cancelRequest` | User | Cancel pending/approved |
| `confirmDelivery` | User | Confirm receipt â flight returning |
| `updateProfile` | User | Edit name/phone/address |
| `approveRequest` | Admin | Approve â decrement stock, return eligible drones |
| `rejectRequest` | Admin | Reject + notify user |
| `assignDrone` | Admin | Pick drone, create flight doc |
| `setWeather` | Admin | Update `weather/current` |
| `restockItem` | Admin | Increment catalog stock |
| `toggleDroneMaintenance` | Admin | Toggle maintenance status |
| `createCatalogItem` | Admin | Create catalog entry |

### Scheduled
- `tickFlights` (every 1 min): advances flights, rolls failure dice (weather 3% in storm, battery 100% if <5%, mechanical 0.2%), 60s delivering hold, auto-completes

### Auth trigger
- `onUserCreated`: provisions `users/{uid}` on first sign-in

### Firestore trigger
- `onFlightWritten`: notifies users on flight status change

---

## Sim engine (`functions/src/lib/sim.ts`)
- Haversine distance (`geo.ts`)
- ETA = `distance / (baseSpeed Ă weatherMod)`
- Battery drain 1.5% per km; fail if <5%
- Progress = `traveledKm / totalKm` per tick
- Returning: reverses origin/destination

---

## Route map

| Page ID | Path | Owner |
|---------|------|-------|
| P-U-01 | `/login` | Belle â â implemented |
| P-U-02 | `/register` | Belle â placeholder |
| P-U-03 | `/user/home` | Bew â placeholder |
| P-U-04 | `/user/queue` | Bew â placeholder |
| **P-U-05** | `/user/tracking/:flightId` | **Poom** â placeholder |
| **P-U-06** | `/user/confirm/:reqId` | **Poom** â placeholder |
| P-U-07 | `/user/history` | Bew â placeholder |
| **P-U-08** | `/user/notifications` | **Poom** â placeholder |
| P-U-09 | `/user/profile` | Belle â placeholder |
| P-A-01 | `/admin/requests` | Bew â placeholder |
| P-A-02 | `/admin/requests/:reqId` | Bew â placeholder |
| P-A-03 | `/admin/drones` | Tawan â placeholder |
| P-A-04 | `/admin/drones/:droneId` | Tawan â placeholder |
| **P-A-05** | `/admin/control` | **Poom** â placeholder |
| P-A-06 | `/admin/weather` | Tawan â placeholder |
| P-A-07 | `/admin/inventory` | Bew â placeholder |

---

## Poom's open issues (all `ready-for-agent`)

| # | Title | Deadline | Notes |
|---|-------|----------|-------|
| **#28** | `flutter_map` spike â throwaway POC | **Day 4 = 2026-05-25 (tomorrow!)** | Prove OSM tiles + go_router + Riverpod compat; custom markers; smooth interpolation. Use `prototype` skill. |
| #11 | P-U-05 Tracking â live map + battery + ETA | â | Stream `flights/{id}`; interpolate drone marker between 60s ticks; battery + ETA chips |
| #12 | P-U-06 Confirm â delivery confirm flow | â | Auto-navigate from tracking when flight hits DELIVERING_HOLD; `confirmDelivery` callable |
| #14 | P-U-08 Notifications â in-app inbox + FCM | â | Stream `users/{uid}/notifications`; FCM token registration (push exempt under emulator) |
| #20 | P-A-05 Admin Control live map | â | "God view" â all active flights; reuses interpolation from P-U-05; blocked until #11 done |

**Issue #28 is due tomorrow.** Start there.

---

## Key source files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `app/lib/core/router.dart` | GoRouter + role redirect guard |
| `app/lib/core/auth/auth_providers.dart` | authStateProvider, userProfileProvider (with retry) |
| `app/lib/core/auth/auth_repository.dart` | Firebase Auth wrapper |
| `app/lib/core/auth/user_profile.dart` | UserProfile model |
| `app/lib/features/auth/login_page.dart` | Login (only fully-implemented page) |
| `functions/src/index.ts` | All function exports |
| `functions/src/lib/sim.ts` | Flight simulation math |
| `functions/src/lib/geo.ts` | Haversine distance |
| `firestore.rules` | Security rules |
| `scripts/dev.ts` | Full-stack dev runner |

---

## Constraints / ADRs

- **ADR-0001**: Native Windows dev for non-lead devs
- **ADR-0002**: "Done" = full features on emulator; FCM push-to-device exempt
- **ADR-0003**: Belle ships shared widget stubs Day 4 â consumers can start in parallel. `DroneMap`, `BatteryBar`, `StatusChip`, `ItemPicker` are Belle's widgets.

---

## Shared widgets Belle owns (stub API frozen Day 4)

- `DroneMap` â flutter_map wrapper with drone marker
- `BatteryBar` â battery percentage visual
- `StatusChip` â request/flight status badge
- `ItemPicker` â catalog item selector

Poom's pages (P-U-05, P-A-05) will consume `DroneMap` and `BatteryBar`.

---

## Demo accounts (debug mode)
- User: `[REDACTED:thai-id]` / `Demo#101`
- Admin: `[REDACTED:thai-id]` / `Admin#001`

---

## Known gaps / limitations
- `tickFlights` doesn't fire under emulator (no pub/sub emulator) â manual trigger needed for demo
- FCM push not delivered on-device under emulator â in-app inbox is the fallback
- No Flutter unit/widget tests yet
- No shared `data/models/` or `data/repositories/` layer created yet (pages will need these)

---

## Recommended next step
Start **issue #28** (`flutter_map` spike, due 2026-05-25). Use `/prototype` skill.
Then implement P-U-05 (#11) â P-U-06 (#12) â P-U-08 (#14) â P-A-05 (#20).

## Why this exists
Pre-implementation orientation. All project .md files, docs/, and backend schema reviewed before writing any code.
No Prisma schema exists â data models live in TypeScript callables + Firestore rules.

---

## Project at a glance

**DroneAid** â drone-based relief-supply delivery simulator. Flutter + Firebase mobile app. War-zone civilians request supplies, admins approve + assign drones, users track live. CSC291 KMUTT group project.

| | |
|---|---|
| Deadline | 2026-06-04 (submission 2026-06-05) |
| Scope | Emulator-only â no real Firebase deploy (ADR-0002) |
| Platform | Android only (iOS/web disabled) |
| Dev env | Native Windows (ADR-0001) |
| Dev command | `bun scripts/dev.ts` (build + emulators + seed + tsc-watch + Flutter) |

---

## Team ownership (RACI)

| Person | GitHub | Domain |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Aok (Lead) | @AokDesu | Backend: Firestore rules, Cloud Functions, sim engine, FCM, seed, CI/CD |
| Belle | @AokDesu(?) | Identity + shared UI: Login, Register, Profile, Settings, shared widgets |
| Bew | @SNOWxSAUSAGES | Request domain: P-U-03..04, P-U-07, P-A-01, P-A-02, P-A-07 |
| **Poom** | **@Mrmo0p** | **Tracking + maps: P-U-05, P-U-06, P-U-08, P-A-05, #28 spike** |
| Tawan | @Tantawan7 | Fleet domain: P-A-03, P-A-04, P-A-06 |

Belle ships shared widgets by **Day 4 (2026-05-25)** â consumers (Bew, Poom, Tawan) unblocked after that (ADR-0003).

---

## Tech stack

| Layer | Choice |
|-------|--------|
| Flutter | 3.22+, Dart 3.4+ |
| State | Riverpod (StreamProvider, FutureProvider) |
| Routing | go_router (role-aware redirect guard) |
| Maps | flutter_map 7.0.2 + OpenStreetMap tiles + latlong2 |
| Auth | Firebase Auth (synthetic email: `{nationalId}@drone-aid.local`) |
| DB | Cloud Firestore |
| Functions | Firebase Functions v6 TypeScript, Node ES2022, asia-southeast1 |
| Validation | Zod (function payloads) |
| Messaging | FCM (in-app inbox under emulator; push-to-device exempt per ADR-0002) |
| Dev runner | Bun + `scripts/dev.ts` |
| Tests | Jest (functions), no Flutter tests yet |

---

## Data model (Firestore â no Prisma)

### `users/{uid}`
`nationalId, name, phone, role("admin"|"user"), deliveryAddress{lat,lng,label}, locked, fcmTokens[], createdAt, lastActivityAt`
Sub: `notifications/{nid}`

### `catalog/{itemId}`
`name, weightKg, stock, icon, active`

### `requests/{reqId}`
`userId, items[{catalogId,qty}], totalWeightKg, deliveryAddress{lat,lng,label}, priority("normal"|"urgent"), notes, status("pending"â"approved"â"in_flight"â"delivered"â"confirmed"|"rejected"|"failed"|"cancelled"), decidedBy, decidedAt, rejectReason, currentFlightId`

### `drones/{droneId}`
`name, baseLocation{lat,lng}, maxPayloadKg, batteryPct, status("idle"|"flying"|"maintenance"|"offline"), currentFlightId`

### `flights/{flightId}`
`droneId, requestId, userId, origin{lat,lng}, destination{lat,lng}, takeoffAt, etaAt, speedKmh, weatherModifierAtTakeoff, batteryAtTakeoff, status("enroute"â"delivering"â"completed"|"returning"âidle), failureType("weather"|"battery"|"mechanical"), archived, deliveringStartedAt, returningStartedAt`

### `weather/current`
`state("clear"|"rain"|"storm")` â speed modifier: clear 1.0Ă, rain 0.85Ă, storm 0.6Ă

---

## Cloud Functions (13 total)

### Callables
| Function | Who | Purpose |
|----------|-----|---------|
| `submitRequest` | User | Create pending request; validate â€6kg + stock |
| `cancelRequest` | User | Cancel pending/approved |
| `confirmDelivery` | User | Confirm receipt â flight returning |
| `updateProfile` | User | Edit name/phone/address |
| `approveRequest` | Admin | Approve â decrement stock, return eligible drones |
| `rejectRequest` | Admin | Reject + notify user |
| `assignDrone` | Admin | Pick drone, create flight doc |
| `setWeather` | Admin | Update `weather/current` |
| `restockItem` | Admin | Increment catalog stock |
| `toggleDroneMaintenance` | Admin | Toggle maintenance status |
| `createCatalogItem` | Admin | Create catalog entry |

### Scheduled
- `tickFlights` (every 1 min): advances flights, rolls failure dice (weather 3% in storm, battery 100% if <5%, mechanical 0.2%), 60s delivering hold, auto-completes

### Auth trigger
- `onUserCreated`: provisions `users/{uid}` on first sign-in

### Firestore trigger
- `onFlightWritten`: notifies users on flight status change

---

## Sim engine (`functions/src/lib/sim.ts`)
- Haversine distance (`geo.ts`)
- ETA = `distance / (baseSpeed Ă weatherMod)`
- Battery drain 1.5% per km; fail if <5%
- Progress = `traveledKm / totalKm` per tick
- Returning: reverses origin/destination

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: E:\ok2\CSC291-DroneAid\.claude\skills\prototype

# Prototype

A prototype is **throwaway code that answers a question**. The question decides the shape.

## Pick a branch

Identify which question is being answered â from the user's prompt, the surrounding code, or by asking if the user is around:

- **"Does this logic / state model feel right?"** â [LOGIC.md](LOGIC.md). Build a tiny interactive terminal app that pushes the state machine through cases that are hard to reason about on paper.
- **"What should this look like?"** â [UI.md](UI.md). Generate several radically different UI variations on a single route, switchable via a URL search param and a floating bottom bar.

The two branches produce very different artifacts â getting this wrong wastes the whole prototype. If the question is genuinely ambiguous and the user isn't reachable, default to whichever branch better matches the surrounding code (a backend module â logic; a page or component â UI) and state the assumption at the top of the prototype.

## Rules that apply to both

1. **Throwaway from day one, and clearly marked as such.** Locate the prototype code close to where it will actually be used (next to the module or page it's prototyping for) so context is obvious â but name it so a casual reader can see it's a prototype, not production. For throwaway UI routes, obey whatever routing convention the project already uses; don't invent a new top-level structure.
2. **One command to run.** Whatever the project's existing task runner supports â `pnpm <name>`, `python <path>`, `bun <path>`, etc. The user must be able to start it without thinking.
3. **No persistence by default.** State lives in memory. Persistence is the thing the prototype is _checking_, not something it should depend on. If the question explicitly involves a database, hit a scratch DB or a local file with a clear "PROTOTYPE â wipe me" name.
4. **Skip the polish.** No tests, no error handling beyond what makes the prototype _runnable_, no abstractions. The point is to learn something fast and then delete it.
5. **Surface the state.** After every action (logic) or on every variant switch (UI), print or render the full relevant state so the user can see what changed.
6. **Delete or absorb when done.** When the prototype has answered its question, either delete it or fold the validated decision into the real code â don't leave it rotting in the repo.

## When done

The _answer_ is the only thing worth keeping from a prototype. Capture it somewhere durable (commit message, ADR, issue, or a `NOTES.md` next to the prototype) along with the question it was answering. If the user is around, that capture is a quick conversation; if not, leave the placeholder so they (or you, on the next pass) can fill in the verdict before deleting the prototype.
Reading full issue #28 before planning.

input.content:
# Plan: Issue #28 â flutter_map spike (throwaway POC)

## Context
Poom's first task. Throwaway spike to prove flutter_map 7.0.2 works on Android emulator before committing to P-U-05 (tracking page) and P-A-05 (control map). Due Day 4 = 2026-05-25. Branch must NOT merge to main.

**Question being answered:** Do OSM tiles load on Android emulator? Can we smoothly interpolate a drone marker between positions (simulating 60s server ticks)?

**Prototype branch:** UI branch (visual map with animated marker)

---

## Files to create (all throwaway, spike branch only)

### `app/lib/spike/main_spike.dart`
Minimal entry point â no Firebase init, no routing, no Riverpod. Just `MaterialApp` â `FlutterMapSpikePage`.

Run with: `flutter run -t lib/spike/main_spike.dart -d <device>`

### `app/lib/spike/flutter_map_spike.dart`
Single-page prototype. Clearly marked `// PROTOTYPE â throwaway for issue #28`.

**Behavior:**
- `FlutterMap` with OSM tile layer (`https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png`)
- Two hardcoded Bangkok waypoints: A = Silom (13.7563, 100.5018), B = Lat Phrao (13.7937, 100.5323) â ~3km apart
- Blue dot at A, green dot at B (static waypoint markers)
- Red airplane icon = drone marker, lerps from AâBâAââŠ on a repeating 2s `AnimationController` with `Curves.easeInOut`
- Bottom card overlay surfaces full state: current lat/lng, progress %, travel direction

**Animation approach:** `AnimationController` (duration: 2s) + `addListener(() => setState)` + linear lat/lng lerp. On `completed` â `reverse()`, on `dismissed` â `forward()`. This exactly mirrors how P-U-05 will interpolate between 60s server ticks.

**flutter_map 7.0.2 API used:**
```dart
TileLayer(
  urlTemplate: 'https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png',
  userAgentPackageName: 'com.droneaid.app',
)
MarkerLayer(markers: [...])
MapOptions(initialCenter: ..., initialZoom: 13.5)
```

---

## Steps

1. `git checkout main && git pull --ff-only`
2. `git checkout -b spike/flutter-map`
3. Create `app/lib/spike/main_spike.dart`
4. Create `app/lib/spike/flutter_map_spike.dart`
5. Run `flutter pub get` (flutter_map already in pubspec, no new deps)
6. Run `flutter run -t lib/spike/main_spike.dart -d <android-emulator>` and verify:
   - [ ] OSM tiles render (not blank/grey)
   - [ ] Drone icon animates smoothly between two points
   - [ ] Waypoint dots visible
   - [ ] State overlay shows correct lat/lng + progress
7. Push branch + open PR (do NOT target main â target a dead-end branch or leave as draft)
8. Post findings as comment on issue #11

---

## Expected gotchas to watch for
- **INTERNET permission**: Already granted via Firebase â should be fine
- **OSM rate-limits on emulator**: May see 429s if tiles hammered; add `maxNativeZoom` or caching layer in P-U-05 proper
- **userAgentPackageName**: Required by flutter_map 7.x; use `com.droneaid.app`
- **Emulator network**: OSM tiles are HTTPS â emulator needs internet. Confirm emulator is not in airplane mode
- **OverlayPane layering**: MarkerLayer renders above TileLayer by default in flutter_map 7 â no z-index tricks needed
- **Animation jank**: If `addListener+setState` is too heavy, consider `AnimatedBuilder` wrapper instead

---

## No files touched in production
This spike lives entirely in `app/lib/spike/` on branch `spike/flutter-map`. Zero changes to `router.dart`, `main.dart`, or any feature code.

---

## Outcome gates
- **Pass** â P-U-05 (#11) and P-A-05 (#20) proceed with flutter_map + interpolation approach. Post findings on #11.
- **Fail** (tiles don't load / animation too jank) â escalate to @AokDesu, adjust P-U-05 scope or use static map fallback.

**Animation approach:** `AnimationController` (duration: 2s) + `addListener(() => setState)` + linear lat/lng lerp. On `completed` â `reverse()`, on `dismissed` â `forward()`. This exactly mirrors how P-U-05 will interpolate between 60s server ticks.

**flutter_map 7.0.2 API used:**
```dart
TileLayer(
  urlTemplate: 'https://tile.openstreetmap.org/{z}/{x}/{y}.png',
  userAgentPackageName: 'com.droneaid.app',
)
MarkerLayer(markers: [...])
MapOptions(initialCenter: ..., initialZoom: 13.5)
```

---

## Steps

1. `git checkout main && git pull --ff-only`
2. `git checkout -b spike/flutter-map`
3. Create `app/lib/spike/main_spike.dart`
4. Create `app/lib/spike/flutter_map_spike.dart`
5. Run `flutter pub get` (flutter_map already in pubspec, no new deps)
6. Run `flutter run -t lib/spike/main_spike.dart -d <android-emulator>` and verify:
   - [ ] OSM tiles render (not blank/grey)
   - [ ] Drone icon animates smoothly between two points
   - [ ] Waypoint dots visible
   - [ ] State overlay shows correct lat/lng + progress
7. Push branch + open PR (do NOT target main â target a dead-end branch or leave as draft)
8. Post findings as comment on issue #11

---

## Expected gotchas to watch for
- **INTERNET permission**: Already granted via Firebase â should be fine
- **OSM rate-limits on emulator**: May see 429s if tiles hammered; add `maxNativeZoom` or caching layer in P-U-05 proper
- **userAgentPackageName**: Required by flutter_map 7.x; use `com.droneaid.app`
- **Emulator network**: OSM tiles are HTTPS â emulator needs internet. Confirm emulator is not in airplane mode
- **OverlayPane layering**: MarkerLayer renders above TileLayer by default in flutter_map 7 â no z-index tricks needed
- **Animation jank**: If `addListener+setState` is too heavy, consider `AnimatedBuilder` wrapper instead

---

## No files touched in production
This spike lives entirely in `app/lib/spike/` on branch `spike/flutter-map`. Zero changes to `router.dart`, `main.dart`, or any feature code.

---

## Outcome gates
- **Pass** â P-U-05 (#11) and P-A-05 (#20) proceed with flutter_map + interpolation approach. Post findings on #11.
- **Fail** (tiles don't load / animation too jank) â escalate to @AokDesu, adjust P-U-05 scope or use static map fallback.
